modules.name: http_header
modules.desc:
HTTP Header (also called HTTP head / HTTP header field / HTTP header fields, etc.) allows clients and servers to pass additional information with an HTTP request or HTTP response.

An HTTP header consists of its case-insensitive name followed by a colon (:) and its value.

Depending on the message context, headers can be divided into:

  • Request headers — contain more information about the resource to be fetched or about the client requesting the resource.
  • Response headers — contain additional information about the response, such as its location or the server providing the response.
  • Representation headers — contain information about the resource body, such as its MIME type or the encoding/compression scheme applied.
  • Payload headers — contain information about the payload data representation that is independent of the representation, including content length and the encoding used for transfer.

See also: Wikipedia (EN) / MDN

modules.name: request_headers
modules.desc:
Request headers contain more information about the resource to be fetched or about the client requesting the resource.

Here are some example request headers from a GET request:

GET /home.html HTTP/1.1
Host: developer.mozilla.org
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10.9; rv:50.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/50.0
Accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,*/*;q=0.8
Accept-Language: en-US,en;q=0.5
Accept-Encoding: gzip, deflate, br
Referer: https://developer.mozilla.org/testpage.html
Connection: keep-alive
Upgrade-Insecure-Requests: 1
If-Modified-Since: Mon, 18 Jul 2016 02:36:04 GMT
If-None-Match: "c561c68d0ba92bbeb8b0fff2a9199f722e3a621a"
Cache-Control: max-age=0

Common Request Header Fields:

Field Name Description Example Status
Accept The acceptable response content types (Content-Types). See Content Negotiation. Accept: text/plain Permanent
Accept-Charset The acceptable character sets. Accept-Charset: utf-8 Permanent
Accept-Encoding The list of acceptable encoding methods. See HTTP Compression. Accept-Encoding: gzip, deflate Permanent
Accept-Language The list of natural languages for the acceptable response content. See Content Negotiation. Accept-Language: en-US Permanent
Accept-Datetime The acceptable version expressed in terms of time. Accept-Datetime: Thu, 31 May 2007 20:35:00 GMT Provisional
Authorization Authentication information for HTTP authentication. Authorization: Basic QWxhZGRpbjpvcGVuIHNlc2FtZQ== Permanent
Cache-Control Directives that must be obeyed by all caching mechanisms along the request/response chain. Cache-Control: no-cache Permanent
Connection The connection type that the browser prefers to use. Connection: keep-alive Connection: Upgrade Permanent
Cookie An HTTP Cookie previously sent by the server via the Set-Cookie header. Cookie: $Version=1; Skin=new; Permanent: Standard
Content-Length The length of the request body in octets (8-bit bytes). Content-Length: 348 Permanent
Content-MD5 The binary MD5 hash of the content of the request body, encoded in Base64. Content-MD5: Q2hlY2sgSW50ZWdyaXR5IQ== Obsolete
Content-Type The MIME type of the request body (used in POST and PUT requests). Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded Permanent
Date The date and time the message was sent (in the "HTTP-date" format defined in RFC 7231). Date: Tue, 15 Nov 1994 08:12:31 GMT Permanent
Expect Indicates that the client expects the server to behave in a specific way. Expect: 100-continue Permanent
From The email address of the user making this request. From: user@example.com Permanent
Host The domain name of the server (for virtual hosting) and the TCP port number on which the server is listening. If the requested port is the standard port for the service, the port number may be omitted. Required since HTTP/1.1. Host: zh.wikipedia.org:80 Host: zh.wikipedia.org Permanent
If-Match Only perform the action if the client-supplied entity matches the corresponding entity on the server. Mainly used with methods like PUT to update a resource only if it has not been modified since the user last retrieved it. If-Match: "737060cd8c284d8af7ad3082f209582d" Permanent
If-Modified-Since Allows a 304 Not Modified response to be returned if the content has not been modified. If-Modified-Since: Sat, 29 Oct 1994 19:43:31 GMT Permanent
If-None-Match Allows a 304 Not Modified response to be returned if the content has not been modified (see HTTP ETag). If-None-Match: "737060cd8c284d8af7ad3082f209582d" Permanent
If-Range If the entity has not been modified, send me the missing part(s); otherwise send the entire new entity. If-Range: "737060cd8c284d8af7ad3082f209582d" Permanent
If-Unmodified-Since Only send the response if the entity has not been modified since a specific time. If-Unmodified-Since: Sat, 29 Oct 1994 19:43:31 GMT Permanent
Max-Forwards Limits the number of times the message can be forwarded by proxies and gateways. Max-Forwards: 10 Permanent
Origin Initiates a request for Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (requires the server to add an 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' field in the response). Origin: http://www.example-social-network.com Permanent: Standard
Pragma Implementation-specific fields that may have various effects at any time in the request/response chain. Pragma: no-cache Permanent but not commonly used
Proxy-Authorization Authentication information for authenticating to a proxy. Proxy-Authorization: Basic QWxhZGRpbjpvcGVuIHNlc2FtZQ== Permanent
Range Requests only a portion of an entity. Byte offsets start at 0. See Byte serving. Range: bytes=500-999 Permanent
Referer The address of the previous web page from which a link to the currently requested page was followed. Referer: http://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page Permanent
TE The transfer encodings the browser is willing to accept: can use the values from the Transfer-Encoding response header; can also use "trailers" (related to chunked transfer) to indicate the browser expects to receive some additional fields after the last zero-sized chunk. TE: trailers, deflate Permanent
User-Agent The browser's user agent string. User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64; rv:12.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/21.0 Permanent
Upgrade Requests the server to upgrade to another protocol. Upgrade: HTTP/2.0, SHTTP/1.3, IRC/6.9, RTA/x11 Permanent
Via Informs the server of the proxies through which the request was sent. Via: 1.0 fred, 1.1 example.com (Apache/1.1) Permanent
Warning A general warning that there may be an error in the entity body. Warning: 199 Miscellaneous warning Permanent
X-Requested-With Mainly used to identify Ajax and XML requests. Most JavaScript frameworks send this field with the value XMLHttpRequest. X-Requested-With: XMLHttpRequest Non-standard
DNT Requests a web application to stop tracking a user. In Firefox, equivalent to the X-Do-Not-Track header field (supported since Firefox/4.0 Beta 11). Safari and Internet Explorer 9 also support this field. DNT: 1 (DNT enabled) DNT: 0 (DNT disabled) Non-standard
X-Forwarded-For A de facto standard for identifying the originating IP address of a client connecting to a web server through an HTTP proxy or load balancer. X-Forwarded-For: client1, proxy1, proxy2 X-Forwarded-For: 129.78.138.66, 129.78.64.103 Non-standard
X-Forwarded-Host A de facto standard for identifying the original Host header sent by the client. X-Forwarded-Host: zh.wikipedia.org:80 X-Forwarded-Host: zh.wikipedia.org Non-standard
X-Forwarded-Proto A de facto standard for identifying the protocol used by the original HTTP request. X-Forwarded-Proto: https Non-standard
Front-End-Https A non-standard header field used by Microsoft servers and load balancers. Front-End-Https: on Non-standard
X-Http-Method-Override Requests a web application to use the method specified in this header field (usually PUT or DELETE) instead of the method specified in the request (usually POST). Useful when a browser or firewall blocks direct PUT or DELETE requests. X-HTTP-Method-Override: DELETE Non-standard
X-ATT-DeviceId Allows the server to more easily parse the common device model and firmware information found in the AT&T device User-Agent field. X-Att-Deviceid: GT-P7320/P7320XXLPG Non-standard
X-Wap-Profile Links to an XML file on the Internet that fully and carefully describes the connecting device. The example on the right is the XML file provided by AT&T for the Samsung Galaxy S2. x-wap-profile: http://wap.samsungmobile.com/uaprof/SGH-I777.xml Non-standard
Proxy-Connection This field originated from an error in early HTTP version implementations. It has exactly the same function as the standard Connection field. Proxy-Connection: keep-alive Non-standard
X-Csrf-Token Used to prevent Cross-Site Request Forgery. Related headers include X-CSRFToken or X-XSRF-TOKEN. X-Csrf-Token: i8XNjC4b8KVok4uw5RftR38Wgp2BFwql Non-standard

modules.name: response_headers
modules.desc:
Response headers contain additional information about the response, such as its location or the server providing it.

Here are some example response headers and representation headers from a GET request:

200 OK
Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *
Connection: Keep-Alive
Content-Encoding: gzip
Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8
Date: Mon, 18 Jul 2016 16:06:00 GMT
Etag: "c561c68d0ba92bbeb8b0f612a9199f722e3a621a"
Keep-Alive: timeout=5, max=997
Last-Modified: Mon, 18 Jul 2016 02:36:04 GMT
Server: Apache
Set-Cookie: mykey=myvalue; expires=Mon, 17-Jul-2017 16:06:00 GMT; Max-Age=31449600; Path=/; secure
Transfer-Encoding: chunked
Vary: Cookie, Accept-Encoding
X-Backend-Server: developer2.webapp.scl3.mozilla.com
X-Cache-Info: not cacheable; meta data too large
X-kuma-revision: 1085259
x-frame-options: DENY

Common Response Header Fields:

Field Name Description Example Status
Access-Control-Allow-Origin Specifies which sites can participate in cross-origin resource sharing. Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * Provisional
Accept-Patch Specifies the file format types supported by the server. Accept-Patch: text/example;charset=utf-8 Permanent
Accept-Ranges What types of partial content range the server supports. Accept-Ranges: bytes Permanent
Age The time in seconds that this object has existed in the proxy cache. Age: 12 Permanent
Allow The valid actions for a specific resource. Used with the HTTP/405 error code. Allow: GET, HEAD Permanent
Cache-Control Informs all caching mechanisms from the server to the client whether they may cache this object. The unit is seconds. Cache-Control: max-age=3600 Permanent
Connection The expected options for this connection. Connection: close Permanent
Content-Disposition A header that allows the client to download a file and suggests a filename. The filename must be wrapped in double quotes. Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="fname.ext" Permanent
Content-Encoding The encoding type used on the data. See HTTP compression. Content-Encoding: gzip Permanent
Content-Language The language used by the content. Content-Language: da Permanent
Content-Length The length of the response body in bytes. Content-Length: 348 Permanent
Content-Location A candidate location for the returned data. Content-Location: /index.htm Permanent
Content-MD5 The binary MD5 hash of the response content, encoded in Base64. Content-MD5: Q2hlY2sgSW50ZWdyaXR5IQ== Obsolete
Content-Range Which part of a complete message this partial message belongs to. Content-Range: bytes 21010-47021/47022 Permanent
Content-Type The MIME type of the current content. Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8 Permanent
Date The date and time this message was sent (in the "HTTP-date" format defined in RFC 7231). Date: Tue, 15 Nov 1994 08:12:31 GMT Permanent
ETag An identifier for a specific version of a resource, usually a message hash. ETag: "737060cd8c284d8af7ad3082f209582d" Permanent
Expires Specifies a date/time after which the response is considered expired. Expires: Thu, 01 Dec 1994 16:00:00 GMT Permanent: Standard
Last-Modified The last modification date of the requested object (in the "HTTP-date" format defined in RFC 7231). Last-Modified: Tue, 15 Nov 1994 12:45:26 GMT Permanent
Link Used to express a typed relationship with another resource, where the type relationship is defined in RFC 5988. Link: </feed>; rel="alternate" Permanent
Location Used for redirection or when a new resource is created. Location: http://www.w3.org/pub/WWW/People.html Permanent
P3P Used to support setting P3P policies. The standard format is "P3P:CP="your_compact_policy"". P3P: CP="This is not a P3P policy! `See http://www.google.com/support/accounts/bin/answer.py?hl=en&answer=151657 for more info."` Permanent
Pragma Implementation-specific fields that may have various effects at any time in the request/response chain. Pragma: no-cache Permanent
Proxy-Authenticate Requires authentication information when accessing a proxy. Proxy-Authenticate: Basic Permanent
Public-Key-Pins Used to mitigate man-in-the-middle attacks by declaring the hash of the TLS certificate used for site authentication. Public-Key-Pins: max-age=2592000; pin-sha256="E9CZ9INDbd+2eRQozYqqbQ2yXLVKB9+xcprMF+44U1g="; Permanent
Refresh Used to set timed redirect jumps. The example on the right sets a jump to "http://www.w3.org/pub/WWW/People.html" after 5 seconds. Refresh: 5; url=http://www.w3.org/pub/WWW/People.html Proprietary, Netscape extension, but supported by most browsers
Retry-After If an entity is temporarily unavailable, this header informs the client when to retry later. The value can be a specific time period (in seconds) or an HTTP date. Example 1: Retry-After: 120 Example 2: Retry-After: Fri, 07 Nov 2014 23:59:59 GMT Permanent
Server The name of the server. Server: Apache/2.4.1 (Unix) Permanent
Set-Cookie An HTTP cookie. Set-Cookie: UserID=JohnDoe; Max-Age=3600; Version=1 Permanent: Standard
Status A Common Gateway Interface header field used to indicate the status of the current HTTP response. Normal HTTP responses use a separate "Status-Line" instead, as defined in RFC 7230. Status: 200 OK -
Strict-Transport-Security This HTTP Strict Transport Security header informs clients how long to cache this mandatory HTTPS policy and whether the policy applies to subdomains. Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=16070400; includeSubDomains Permanent: Standard
Trailer This header value indicates that this series of headers is encoded by chunked transfer encoding. Trailer: Max-Forwards Permanent
Transfer-Encoding The encoding form used to safely transfer the entity to the user. Currently defined methods include: chunked, compress, deflate, gzip, and identity. Transfer-Encoding: chunked Permanent
Upgrade Requests the client to upgrade to another protocol. Upgrade: HTTP/2.0, SHTTP/1.3, IRC/6.9, RTA/x11 Permanent
Vary Informs downstream proxy servers how to match future request headers to decide whether cached response content can be used instead of requesting new content from the origin server. Vary: * Permanent
Via Informs the proxy server's client of the path through which the current response was sent. Via: 1.0 fred, 1.1 example.com (Apache/1.1) Permanent
Warning A general warning that there may be an error in the entity body. Warning: 199 Miscellaneous warning Permanent
WWW-Authenticate Indicates the authentication scheme that should be used when requesting this entity. WWW-Authenticate: Basic Permanent
X-Frame-Options Clickjacking protection. deny: This page may not be displayed in a frame, even within the same domain. sameorigin: This page may be displayed in a frame within the same domain. allow-from *uri*: This page may be displayed in a frame at the specified URI. allowall: Allows frames from any location (non-standard value). X-Frame-Options: deny Obsolete
X-XSS-Protection Cross-site scripting (XSS) filter. X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block Non-standard
Content-Security-Policy, X-Content-Security-Policy, X-WebKit-CSP Content Security Policy definition. X-WebKit-CSP: default-src 'self' Non-standard
X-Content-Type-Options The only allowed value is "nosniff", which prevents Internet Explorer from MIME-sniffing files. This also applies when Google Chrome downloads extensions. X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff Non-standard
X-Powered-By Indicates the technology used to support the current web application (e.g., PHP). Version details are usually placed in X-Runtime or X-Version. X-Powered-By: PHP/5.4.0 Non-standard
X-UA-Compatible Recommends a specific rendering engine (usually in backward-compatibility mode) to display the content. Also used to activate Chrome Frame in Internet Explorer. X-UA-Compatible: IE=EmulateIE7 X-UA-Compatible: IE=edge X-UA-Compatible: Chrome=1 Non-standard
X-Content-Duration Specifies the length of audio/video in seconds. Only supported by Gecko-based browsers. X-Content-Duration: 42.666 Non-standard
Feature-Policy Controls specific APIs. Feature-Policy: vibrate 'none'; geolocation 'none' Non-standard
Permissions-Policy Controls specific APIs as a W3C standard, replacing Feature-Policy. Permissions-Policy: microphone=(),geolocation=(),camera=() Non-standard
X-Permitted-Cross-Domain-Policies Flash cross-site attack protection. X-Permitted-Cross-Domain-Policies: none Non-standard
Referrer-Policy Information leakage protection. Referrer-Policy: origin-when-cross-origin Non-standard
Expect-CT Prevents SSL spoofing. The unit is seconds. Expect-CT: max-age=31536000, enforce Non-standard
